Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Tatjana Smith aims for unique HEYNS history at the 2024 Paris Olympics

The double is on the cards.

Tatjana Smith is gunning for a unique slice of South African swimming history at the Paris 2024 Olympics.

Smithstands to become only the second swimmer in history after Penny Heyns in 1996 to complete the 100m and 200m breaststroke golden double at the same Olympics event. She wasn’t yet born when Heyns accomplished this historic feat. The 27-year-old contests her 200m breaststroke heat onWednesday 31 July at 11:00 SA time. Thesemi-finals go at 21:51 and 21:58.The finalis onThursday 01 August at 21:04.You know thatNando’s will be watching. Smith won this event in Tokyo with a time of2.18.95, a world record which has since been eclipsed by Russian Evgeniia Chikunova in 2023 with a time of2.17.55.Will Smith take her record back?

Smith touched the wall first in1.05.28in the 100m breaststroke thanks to amassive surge in the final quarter of the race.China’s Tang Qianting scooped silver and a visibly delighted Mona McSharry, from Ireland, got the bronze.

“I had no idea where I was, literally most of the race,” admitted Smith afterwards.

“I actually didn’t think I medalled because when I turned to my left, the two blocks next to me both had lights on and it felt like someone else on the other side touched first.

“So I actually didn’t think I was medalling.

“My main goal was just to not do what I did in Tokyo and look around, so literally the last 15 metres, I was like, no, close your eyes, let’s just go.”

“I swam for everyone that’s been there for me, that’s believed with me, for our country as well.”

Tatjana is just the fifth South African to win an individual Olympic swimming title after Joan Harrison in 1952, Penny Heynes in 1996, Cameron van der Burgh in 2012 and Chad le Clos in 2012). She regards Heyns and Le Clos as sporting heroes, along with iconic Olympian pair Michael Phelps and Usain Bolt.

title: Tatjana Smith aims for HEYNS history at the 2024 Paris Olympics
author: James Freemantle
description: Tatjana Smith is gunning for a unique slice of South African swimming history at the Paris 2024 Olympics on Wednesday and Thursday.
